name: Suspicious DNS Query Length
notes: This rule triggers when a DNS is abnormally long, this could indicate DGA domains & onion domains.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: DNS, BB:DeviceDefinition: Web Servers
when the event category for the event is one of the following Application.DNS In Progress, Application.Request Successful, Application.Request In Progress
when the event matches STRLEN(URL Host) > 40 AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious DNS Query Length on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: DNS, BB:DeviceDefinition: Web Servers
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Application.DNS In Progress, Application.Request Successful, Application.Request In Progress
AND when the event matches STRLEN(URL Host) > 40 AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious DNS query length
Event description: An unusually long DNS request has been issued.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
